summary: Frontend-only end-to-end testing of Minnerva Innov marketing site (static React app). Verified routes, navigation, hero CTAs, case study cards & detail pages (incl. YouTube embed on Case 2), services page (4 blocks + #foresight anchor), contact form mailto + toasts, stakeholder tabs (R&D/Marketing/CEO panel switching), mobile nav toggle, and custom cursor on desktop. All 11 requested feature areas pass. Only 1 issue found: hero background video URL returns 404 (broken external pixabay asset).

ui_bugs.issue: Hero background <video> source returns HTTP 404. URL https://cdn.pixabay.com/video/2023/11/05/187974-881867892_large.mp4 is dead — the hero shows only the dark gradient with no looping lab video, defeating the intended royalty-free lab aesthetic. Console shows 'Failed to load resource: 404'.

action_items: Replace the broken hero video URL in /app/frontend/src/components/sections/Hero.jsx (line 44). Suggest either uploading a royalty-free lab MP4 to /app/frontend/public/videos/ and referencing it locally, or picking another verified Pixabay/Coverr/Pexels CDN URL that resolves with HTTP 200. Optionally add a poster image fallback so the hero never looks empty if the video fails.
critical_code_review_comments: Hero.jsx: external video has no <poster> fallback and no onError handler — when CDN 404s, the area shows only black. Provide a local fallback or poster.

ContactPage.jsx: form submit relies on window.location.href = mailto: which can silently no-op in some browsers if no mail handler is registered; consider also exposing a copy-to-clipboard fallback (non-blocking).
All testid coverage is excellent (hero-cta-*, case-card-*, service-block-*, stakeholder-tab/panel-*, contact-input-*, contact-submit, nav-link-*, nav-mobile-toggle, footer-*).
StakeholderMoments uses AnimatePresence mode="wait" with 0.5s exit + 0.5s enter — total ~1s per tab swap. Functionally correct but feels slow for a 3-tab UI; consider 0.25s.
No code-splitting/lazy loading on case study & services pages — fine for current size but worth adding React.lazy when content grows.
test_credentials: N/A — public marketing site, no auth.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All requested flows verified on https://innovation-lab-54.preview.emergentagent.com at 1440x900 (and 390x844 for mobile nav). Verified: 3 hero CTAs (innovate/differentiate/move-fast), 3 case cards, 4 service blocks, 3 stakeholder tabs (panel switches correctly when waiting >=1s for Framer Motion AnimatePresence transition — earlier 400ms wait gave a false 'stuck on rd' reading; not a bug), Case 2 (million-dollar-smiles) has YouTube iframe, contact form shows error toast 'Name, email and challenge are required' on empty submit and success toast 'Opening your email client…' + builds mailto URL on valid submit (mailto navigation could not be programmatically captured because location.href setter cannot be redefined, but toast confirmation + code path verified). Mobile nav toggle works, custom cursor (.mn-cursor + .mn-cursor-ring) renders on desktop. Only network failure across all pages: 404 on the hero pixabay video.
rca_of_the_issue: Hero pixabay video 404: the URL https://cdn.pixabay.com/video/2023/11/05/187974-881867892_large.mp4 was likely guessed/fabricated rather than verified — Pixabay's video CDN paths require valid asset IDs. Reproduction: open / and check Network tab — request to that URL returns 404. Mitigation: (a) host a local MP4 in /app/frontend/public/videos/lab.mp4 and reference src="/videos/lab.mp4", or (b) use a verified URL from coverr.co / pexels.com / a real pixabay asset, plus add poster="/images/hero-poster.jpg" and onError handler to hide the <video> when the source fails.
